commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Neither say they in their heart, Let us now fear the LORD our God, who gives rain, both the former and the latter, in its season: he reserves unto us the appointed weeks of the harvest. Rain, in autumn and in spring, Deuteronomy xi. (Calmet) Fulness. Hebrew, "the weeks for harvest. "(Haydock) That of barley began at the Passover, and that of wheat ended before the feast of weeks, (Exodus xxxiv. , and Leviticus xxiii. ; Calmet) or Pentecost.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
